name: House Without a Key
tagline: Sunset hula under a century-old kiawe tree at the Halekulani — one of the most magical dining experiences in Hawaii.
mustOrder: Halekulani coconut cake — the hotel's signature since 1984
any fresh catch with a mai tai at sunset
pupu platter to share while watching the hula dancer
parking: Halekulani valet ($45+) or street parking on Saratoga/Kalia Rd
hours: Breakfast 7–11am, Lunch 11:30am–2pm, Dinner 5–9pm. Hula nightly at 5:30pm (no cover)
bestTime: Arrive by 5pm for a good seat near the hula stage before the 5:30 performance. Sunset timing is everything
talkStory: Named after the 1925 Earl Derr Biggers novel (which also created Charlie Chan), House Without a Key is the Halekulani's open-air oceanfront restaurant. The nightly hula performance under the kiawe tree — featuring former Miss Hawaii winners — is one of those only-in-Hawaii moments that genuinely lives up to the fantasy. It's not a luau show; it's a single elegant dancer performing traditional hula while a Hawaiian steel guitar trio plays. The food is solid upscale Hawaiian (not the star — the setting is), and the cocktail program is excellent. Even if you can't afford a Halekulani room, a sunset drink here is the move. Named one of the most romantic restaurant settings in the US by multiple publications.
